name: textgrad
prompt:
Here is the role of the variable you will improve: <ROLE>Retrieval pipeline configuration. Modify == CURRENT CONFIG == values to maximise RAGAS scores.</ROLE>.

The variable is the text within the following span: <VARIABLE> == PIPELINE ARCHITECTURE ==

This is a 3-layer academic paper retrieval (...) CURRENT CONFIG ==
top_k: 3
rrf_k: 60
gist_lambda: 0.7000
bm25_min_score: 0.0000
dense_min_similarity: 0.0000
colbert_min_score: 0.0000
cross_encoder_min_score: 0.0000 </VARIABLE>

Here is the context and feedback we got for the variable:

<CONTEXT></CONTEXT>

Improve the variable (Retrieval pipeline configuration. Modify == CURRENT CONFIG == values to maximise RAGAS scores.) using the feedback provided in <FEEDBACK> tags.
You must follow the following constraints:

<CONSTRAINTS>Constraint 1: Only change numeric values in == CURRENT CONFIG ==.
Constraint 2: Do not rename or add keys.
Constraint 3: Keep top_k between 3 and 20.
Constraint 4: Keep rrf_k between 10 and 120.
Constraint 5: Keep gist_lambda between 0.0 and 1.0.
Constraint 6: Keep all min_score / min_similarity values between 0.0 and 2.0.</CONSTRAINTS>

Send the improved variable in the following format:

<IMPROVED_VARIABLE>{the improved variable}</IMPROVED_VARIABLE>

Send ONLY the improved variable between the <IMPROVED_VARIABLE> tags, and nothing else.

name: textgrad
optimizer.response:
<IMPROVED_VARIABLE> == PIPELINE ARCHITECTURE ==

This is a 3-layer academic paper retrieval (...) CURRENT CONFIG ==
top_k: 12
rrf_k: 100
gist_lambda: 0.8500
bm25_min_score: 0.1000
dense_min_similarity: 0.3000
colbert_min_score: 0.1500
cross_encoder_min_score: 0.2500 </IMPROVED_VARIABLE>
